caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied 54171 occupation proxy includes biotech-weighted roles and omits much of the engineering/test tail of 541715.
inputs.pi_dist: No public contract-type mix was located for lower-middle-market 541715 targets; this is a portfolio mix assumption.
inputs.pi_moat: SBA affiliation rules are a risk to some targets rather than evidence that every 541715 firm has a small-business moat.
inputs.t50_years: BTOS is economy-wide/professional-services evidence, not a 541715 adoption survey.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: The observed statistic is national, not an exact 541715 rate.
inputs.historical_analogs: No direct historical-analog dataset is asserted.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: No NAICS- and EBITDA-band-specific owner-age source was located; this input requires target-level verification.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: This deliberately withholds the succession uplift.
inputs.active_consolidators: KippsDeSanto covers a broader deal market and does not publish an exact 541715 lower-middle-market buyer count.
inputs.buy_mult: No observed $1–10M EBITDA 541715 transaction series was available; sources are broad market comparables, not direct comps.
inputs.exit_mult: Exit outcome depends on backlog, clearance, customer concentration and small-business eligibility; it is not an observed 541715 median.
inputs.pricing_structure: FAR is a contract-framework source, not a revenue-mix survey for 541715.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $344,036 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541715); class <5: 5,421 firms, avg revenue/firm $586,174 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $91,736 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 1,423 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,821,285 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $285,031 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 933 firms, avg revenue/firm $4,064,385 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $636,076 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 1,197 firms, avg revenue/firm $12,825,023 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,007,116 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 519 firms, avg revenue/firm $58,594,915 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $9,170,104 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 549 firms, avg revenue/firm $257,196,235 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $40,251,211 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 1,716

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Partial automation of analysis, software, reporting and administrative work is offset by physical testing, facilities, classified access and sponsor accountability.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541715 ($89,539,535,429) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541715 ($196,524,454,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics, identity, dataset injection, fresh finalization, memo rendering, digests and arithmetic all pass. The useful conditional thesis is directionally supported, with no reversed factor semantics or material capture/terminal double count. Publication requires all authored caveats because the role mix, contract mix, adoption bridge, owner age, buyer count and multiples are disclosed proxies or estimates rather than direct 541715 lower-middle-market observations.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS definition: 541715
sources.what_it_supports: The exact 541715 scope and its broad physical, engineering and life-sciences experimental-development population, excluding nanotechnology and biotechnology.

sources.title: FAR Subpart 16.3 — Cost-Reimbursement Contracts
sources.what_it_supports: Cost-reimbursement payment of allowable incurred costs; cost-plus-fixed-fee use in R&D and its fixed-fee incentive structure.

sources.title: SBA Size Standards
sources.what_it_supports: Small-business contracting eligibility and the requirement to include affiliates' employees or receipts in a size calculation.

sources.title: Aerospace / Defense & Government Services 2025 M&A Survey
sources.what_it_supports: Existence of an active 2025 A&D/government-services deal-market survey; used only as directional comparability evidence.

sources.title: The Microstructure of AI Diffusion: Evidence From Firms, Business Functions, and Worker Tasks
sources.what_it_supports: National BTOS AI-use levels, concentration in knowledge-intensive professional services, and limited functional breadth among adopters.

sources.title: Middle Market M&A Valuations Index
sources.what_it_supports: A broad middle-market reference point: surveyed typical and premium 2026 EBITDA multiples of 6.8x and 9.8x, respectively; not an R&D NAICS comp.

title: Research and Development in the Physical, Engineering, and Life Sciences (except Nanotechnology and Biotechnology)
